what_it_does: A session-handoff note written by one Claude session to a future/past version of itself (post time-travel), explaining that Tim is running several parallel sessions as deliberate triangulation rays on one design object (the geometric 'seed' and identity/DB system), and pointing to the on-disk files that hold the missing context.
observations: Frames the parallel sessions as 'deliberate rays of one triangulation' — Tim sends each session a part, lets it regenerate from its angle, and agreement locates truth, differences are signal.

Lists concrete files: THE-SEED-geometric-substrate.md, SEED-GROWTH-ROUNDS.md, THE-SEED-geldard-lattice.md (sibling rendering using quadtree/Morton-Z-order, dyadic doubling, log-polar + conformal z->z^k), CONVERGENCE-OBJECT.md, ORIGIN-MEMORY-AMPLIFICATION.md and others.

Summarizes the Convergence Law (every unit has a structural and semantic position; their distance = resistance = wasted compute until repaired; 'laws are resistance-removal — paid once, dividending forever').

Summarizes the Classification Law (tiny-model judgment = one axis, two extremes, 3-5 divisions; bigger discriminations = progressive chains) and the Barrier Protocol (Tim+agent as two coordinate systems; agent=amplifier).
References the `recollection` episodic-memory plugin as the first concrete instance of collective-memory merge, consuming the Convergence Object.
Contains a 'Live-work collisions' lesson: before editing any services.json / shared registry row, READ THE FULL EXISTING ROW (author admits clobbering notes/ids and correcting in commit 09b043a).
Documents embed-pplx (perplexity pplx-embed-context-v1-4b via custom transformers server) and embed-jina-v4 (jina-embeddings-v4 via serve_jina_v4.py) as live embedders.
States ops/model_capabilities.json is the canonical capability catalog (model-id keyed, {value,source}, ~48 models at the future point) guarded by named acceptance tests.

Restates the identity/DB design: store positions, compute perspectives; store = points + moves + grammar + event journal + origins/axes registries + origin lineage field; the ONE collapse into resolve(centre, spaces, n, k, audience, exhale).
Explicitly notes NONE of the design is ratified by Tim — 'expand-before-harden, awaiting his part-by-part reaction.'
standouts: The parallel-session triangulation method ('rays of one object, agreement locates truth, differences are signal') as Tim's deliberate working method.
The Convergence Law framing resistance = distance between structural and semantic position = wasted compute until repaired.
The operational lesson about reading full registry rows before editing to avoid cross-session contamination (commit 09b043a).
conventions: Working-doc register under build-prep/brain/ — narrative handoff prose, not the strict self-description schema.
Heavy cross-referencing to sibling design files by path.
concerns: Author self-reports having added wrong notes/ids onto another session's live work — flags a real cross-session collision risk on shared registries.
notes: A self-to-self handoff that orients a session inside Tim's deliberate parallel-session triangulation on the seed/identity-DB design, with file pointers and a hard lesson about shared-registry edits.
questions: Has Tim since ratified any of the resolve(centre, spaces, n, k, audience, exhale) collapse described as unratified here?
What is the current (post-handoff) state of the model_capabilities.json catalog the note describes at a 'future' point?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — it is explicitly a message to a future/past self orienting it to parallel context, and that is exactly its content.
apparent_themes: triangulation across parallel sessions (multiple rays, one object)
the seed as a geometric/coordinate substrate (quadtree, log-polar, conformal map)
intention_signals: Trying to prevent context loss and cross-session collision by transmitting, in one place, the shared design object, the co-ray relationship between sessions, and the operational discipline needed when many sessions touch shared state.
novelty: The explicit articulation of a deliberate human-run multi-session triangulation method, and the convergence/resistance vocabulary linking structural and semantic position.
contribution: Contributes the cross-session orientation map for the seed/identity-DB design effort — the catalog of sibling design files, the Convergence/Classification/Barrier laws in brief, the live embedder/catalog state, and the resolve-collapse design intent, plus the shared-registry editing discipline.
summary_for_embedding: A self-to-self session handoff describing Tim's parallel-session triangulation on the geometric seed and identity/DB design, pointing to sibling design files and the Convergence/Classification laws.
intention_for_embedding: To orient a (time-traveled) session inside the parallel triangulation effort and carry forward the unratified identity/DB design and a lesson against clobbering shared registry rows.
